name: DeFi Prime Brokerage
description: Smart-contract enforced lending with per-venue, per-asset constraints. Solves the Archegos problem — borrowers can't move capital outside defined parameters. Risk controls in code, not compliance teams finding out after the fact.
das_signal: Multicoin thesis: 'A DeFi prime brokerage can lend against a user's position and enforce through smart contracts exactly which venues the funds can be used on.'

name: Branded Stablecoin Platform
description: Issuance + compliance + yield management platform for fintechs launching their own branded stablecoins. Every fintech wants the float economics that Western Union gets from USDPT — build the Stripe for branded stablecoins.
source: DAS NYC 2026 — Inside Western Union's Stablecoin-Fueled Future
das_signal: WU CEO: 'If I can free up a couple billion dollars of capital, I can buy back half the company.' Citi forecasts $4T stablecoin market.

name: Emerging Market Currency Stablecoins
description: Liquid onchain versions of longtail EM currencies — BRL, MXN, INR, NGN. No liquid onchain versions exist today despite massive demand. $7T/day FX market, EM corridors are the highest-margin segments.
source: DAS NYC 2026 — On the Design Space for Equity, Commodity, and FX Perps
das_signal: Multicoin: 'The real opportunity is longtail emerging market currencies. There aren't really liquid onchain versions of these currencies yet, but there is clearly demand.'

name: Equity Perps on Solana
description: Perpetual futures on public equities (AAPL, TSLA, SPY) settled in stablecoins on Solana. Hyperliquid does $5B/day in equity perps — Solana has zero. 24/7 access, no brokerage required.
source: DAS NYC 2026 — On the Design Space for Equity, Commodity, and FX Perps
das_signal: HL equity perps $5B/day. Traders worldwide traded oil exposure on HL over a weekend during Iran conflict — no other venue offers this.

name: Commodity Perps on Solana
description: Perpetual futures on gold, silver, oil, and other commodities on Solana. Hyperliquid's HIP-3 silver spreads are already tighter than CME (2.4bps vs 3bps). 24/7 settlement in stablecoins.
source: DAS NYC 2026 — Hyperliquid: The Architecture of Onchain Markets
das_signal: Blockworks Research: HL silver median spread 2.4bps (tighter than CME micro silver at 3bps). HIP-3 RWA OI >$1.5B.

name: FX Perps DEX
description: Synthetic FX perpetuals combined with non-USD stablecoin swaps. Trade currency risk 24/7 without bank accounts. Combine with EM stablecoin issuance for full-stack FX on-chain.
source: DAS NYC 2026 — On the Design Space for Equity, Commodity, and FX Perps
das_signal: $7T/day traditional FX market. Most onchain FX today is synthetic. Teams like Hibachi and Open FX working on early versions.

name: Direct Indexing Platform
description: Tokenized index funds (S&P 500, sector indices) with direct indexing — users own the underlying tokenized equities, not a fund wrapper. The Wealthfront for tokenized equities.
source: DAS NYC 2026 — Tokenization's Next Unlock: Direct Indexing for All
das_signal: Dedicated DAS session. S&P Digital Markets 50 index token launched via Dinari. VanEk product team building tokenized index offerings.

name: Tokenized Private Credit Marketplace
description: Onchain marketplace for private credit — the 2nd fastest growing RWA category after treasuries. Bilateral debt markets are fragmented with no centralized infra to fight, making them ideal for blockchain leapfrogging.
das_signal: Multicoin: 'Private credit maps well to onchain because debt markets are fragmented and relationship-driven. No centralized infrastructure to fight against.'

name: Defined-Loss Structured Products
description: On-chain call spreads, put spreads, and defined-risk option structures. Post Oct-10 2025 cascade ($19B wiped, 1.6M liquidated), institutions are structurally shifting from linear perps to defined-loss instruments.
source: DAS NYC 2026 — Avoiding Another 10/10: The Market Structure We Need
das_signal: FalconX and STS Digital both confirmed institutional shift to defined-loss structures. No onchain venue offers this natively on Solana.

name: ADL Replacement Protocol
description: Replace auto-deleveraging (ADL) with an institutional-grade mechanism. ADL is a BitMEX-era artifact that forcibly closes profitable positions during liquidation cascades. No venue has proposed a replacement.
source: DAS NYC 2026 — Avoiding Another 10/10: The Market Structure We Need
das_signal: Chris Long (DRW): 'ADL is just not institutional.' CME uses T+1 margin calls — crypto hasn't decided between 24/7 ADL or institutional T+1.

name: Cross-Margined Prediction Markets
description: Prediction markets cross-margined with perps on a single venue. Bet on events while maintaining perp positions with shared collateral. HIP-4 is bringing this to Hyperliquid — build it on Solana first.
das_signal: HIP-4 coming to HL in 2026. No other venue globally offers cross-margined prediction markets + perps.

name: DeFi Middleware API Layer
description: APIs connecting fintech frontends (Phantom, Kraken, Robinhood) to backend DeFi protocols (Kamino, Drift, Aave). The plumbing layer of the DeFi Mullet — where fintechs plug into crypto without touching complexity.
das_signal: Multicoin DeFi Mullet thesis: frontends → protocols → middleware. yield.xyz, Lei, fun.xyz cited as early examples. Kraken DeFi Earn $150M in 2 months via this model.

name: Agent Policy Engine
description: Operational controls for autonomous agent wallets — spending limits, per-vendor rules, human-in-the-loop approvals, fraud detection. Different from compliance (KYC/AML) — this is runtime governance for agents that hold money.
source: DAS NYC 2026 — Agentic Commerce: What's Real & What's Sci-Fi
das_signal: Privy/Stripe: 120M deployed wallets, 35% of incoming devs building agentic products. 'You don't want to give a credit card to an agent without a lot of control.'

name: Physical Collectibles Marketplace
description: On-chain marketplace for physical collectibles — fine wine, watches, trading cards, art. Fix custody, ownership records, and settlement. Enable collateral use. $300B industry fragmented across eBay, dealers, auction houses.
das_signal: Multicoin BECKOM thesis: 'Collectibles industry is over $300B. Blockchain-enabled marketplaces fix custody, ownership, and settlement.' Portfolio company Baxis already lending against collectibles.

name: Collateral Haircut Engine
description: Real-time collateral valuation engine that haircuts LSTs and stablecoins based on actual liquidity depth, not thin orderbook marks. Post Oct-10 cascade, LSTs valued at orderbook prices amplified liquidations reflexively.
source: DAS NYC 2026 — Avoiding Another 10/10: The Market Structure We Need
das_signal: Referenced in nearly every market-structure panel. Fix: haircut + underlying-asset price feeds instead of orderbook marks for collateral valuation.
